title: Project Scope & Key Hypothesis
purpose: Define market boundaries and the working hypothesis before evidence starts.
evidence:
Scope:
- Vietnam gaming market only
- Mobile and PC only
- Mid-core and casual only; exclude hypercasual and hybridcasual

Hypothesis:
- Opportunity window may emerge over the next 1-3 years.
visual: Two-card layout: scope on the left, hypothesis on the right.
takeaway: This project is not a generic gaming overview; it is a focused sourcing decision exercise.
notes: Derived from outline note and hypothesis rows.

title: Historical Overall Demand
purpose: Measure the long-run size and growth of game demand in Vietnam across mobile and Steam-relevant PC demand.
evidence:
From outline:
- Downloads, DAU, revenue from 2010-2025
- Mobile via Sensor Tower
- Steam estimate for Vietnam
- Commentary on major phases
visual: Three-panel trend chart for downloads, DAU, and revenue.
takeaway: Demand trend should show whether Vietnam is still ramping structurally or maturing into a steadier market.
notes: Key challenge from outline: estimate VN metrics for Steam.

title: Supply-side Factors
purpose: Start the supply-side subsection after the demand wrap-up.
visual: Subsection cover slide.
takeaway: Shift from demand conditions into the policy, support, and capability conditions shaping VN game supply.

title: SHIFTING GOVERNMENT STANCE & INDUSTRY REGULATION
purpose: Show how the state moved from mainly controlling games to formally recognizing them as a strategic cultural-tech industry.
evidence:
Research synthesis:
- policy moved from restriction-first to promotion + regulation
- Decree 147/2024 raises compliance demands but also formalizes the market
- late-2025 cultural-industry designation legitimizes gaming further
visual: Policy timeline plus compare/contrast between heavier regulation and stronger official endorsement.
takeaway: Regulation is stricter than before, but the net direction is more supportive because the state now wants a formal domestic industry, not just containment.
notes: Based on Vietnamese Game Development Government Support.docx

title: SUPPORT FOR DOMESTIC GAME DEVELOPMENT: DIRECT & INDIRECT
purpose: Map the real support stack around local game development, including direct programs, tax treatment, ecosystem events, and the emerging talent pipeline.
evidence:
Research synthesis:
- Resolutions 79/80 and GameVerse/GameHub provide direct ecosystem support
- tax + software-industry treatment provide strong indirect incentives
- PTIT, UIT/VNG/Roblox, RMIT, FPT Polytechnic, SAMA build talent supply
visual: Two-column support map with highlight metric and talent-pipeline block.
takeaway: The state is not simply loosening rules; it is creating multiple layers of support that reduce friction for domestic developers over time.
notes: Based on Vietnamese Game Development Government Support.docx

subsection: Quality of Made in VN Games / Capability of VN Studios
title: QUALITY OF MADE IN VN GAMES / CAPABILITY OF VN STUDIOS
purpose: Show that Vietnam already has credible studio capability, with evidence across outsourcing, mobile publishing, premium PC, and global innovation.
evidence:
Research synthesis:
- Glass Egg + Gameloft built training and process discipline
- Flappy Bird catalyzed mobile confidence
- Amanotes, ABI/Onesoft, Zitga, Sky Mavis show scaled export capability
- premium-PC examples show capability breadth widening
visual: Capability evolution timeline plus flagship proof points and ecosystem KPIs.
takeaway: Vietnam is no longer a raw-talent story only; it has repeatable production capability, though most of it still points outward to export markets first.
notes: Based on Vietnam Game Production Ecosystem_ Evolution.docx

title: Historical Overall Supply
purpose: Show how many new games are entering Vietnam and whether supply is crowding the market.
evidence:
From outline:
- Mobile new releases on Vietnamese stores
- Officially licensed games
- Steam global new releases for reference
- ABEI licensing crawl input
visual: Supply trend line, licensed vs. total bars, Steam reference panel.
takeaway: Rising supply alone is not enough; the key is whether quality supply is growing fast enough to absorb demand.
notes: Key challenge from outline: crawl https://giayphep.abei.gov.vn/.

title: Forecasted Overall Supply
purpose: Estimate the next 1-3 years of release volume and licensing activity.
evidence: From outline:\n- Qualitative forecast with autoregression model\n- Confirmation or adjustment using prior factors
visual: Historical + forecast bars with annotation on regulatory or studio drivers.
takeaway: A moderate supply ramp may still leave room if demand and quality expectations grow faster.

title: Conclusion
purpose: Start the closing recommendation section.
visual: Section break slide.
takeaway: Close with a direct recommendation and practical next steps.

title: Conclusion: does Vietnam offer a sharp sourcing opportunity in 2026?
purpose: Wrap the deck with a direct answer, supporting logic, risks, and recommendation.
evidence:
From outline:
- Whether there is going to be strong opportunity in VN gaming market for the next 1-3 years in mid-core/casual genres
- Increasing demand and ready conditions for adoption of high-quality Made in VN games
- Low supply as most VN studios focus on hypercasual/hybridcasual and global market
- Key challenge: Game development + LiveOps able to compete with international developers
visual: Final recommendation page with confidence level, proof points, and next steps.
takeaway: The final call should clearly state whether Vietnam deserves focused sourcing attention in the next 1-3 years.
